overview: This plant is a Zamia Fern (Bowenia spectabilis), a type of cycad native to Queensland, Australia. It is often mistaken for a true fern due to its lush, compound leaves, but it is actually a non-flowering ancient plant species. The plant thrives in deep shade and wet lowland forests. While it has a subterranean stem, only the glossy green leaves are typically visible above ground level. iNaturalist +4 5 sites Zamia Fern (Bowenia spectabilis) · iNaturalist
